message:
test(readme): CsvOnBadLines + ToTimedeltaErrors variant coverage

Self-review: 6 enum variants across 2 enums had zero integration test
coverage despite each having observable behavioral differences.

Adds readme_bad_lines_and_timedelta_errors — reachable via prelude
alone — that drives:

CsvOnBadLines (3 variants):
- Skip: silently drops bad row (4-field row in 3-column CSV) → 2 rows
- Warn: same row count as Skip (with possible diagnostic to stderr)
- Error: returns Err on the malformed row

ToTimedeltaErrors (3 variants):
- Coerce: bad input → NaT (Null) or Timedelta64(i64::MIN); length preserved
- Raise: returns Err on unparseable input
- Ignore: parse failures don't error; length preserved

Two API discoveries (corrected before commit):

1. CsvOnBadLines::Skip only triggers on EXTRA-field rows. Short rows
   (fewer fields than header) are preserved with missing values per
   pandas semantics — see fp-io test
   csv_on_bad_lines_skip_preserves_short_rows_as_missing.
2. ToTimedeltaErrors::Coerce represents NaT either as Scalar::Null
   or Scalar::Timedelta64(i64::MIN) (Timedelta::NAT). The test
   accepts both representations.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.31.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.29","title":"test(readme): JoinType (Left/Right/Outer/Cross) + MergeValidateMode (OneToMany/ManyToOne/ManyToMany) coverage","description":"Self-review of join surface: only JoinType::Inner and MergeValidateMode::OneToOne are exercised by integration tests. The 4 other JoinType variants (Left, Right, Outer, Cross) and the 3 other MergeValidateMode variants (OneToMany, ManyToOne, ManyToMany) have ZERO coverage despite being core pandas merge surface.

A regression in any of these variants — for example, Outer-join row count or ManyToMany validation behavior — would not surface.

Plan: add readme_join_type_round_trip:
- For each JoinType: build df1+df2 with overlapping keys, run merge_dataframes_on, assert expected row count
- For each MergeValidateMode: drive merge_with_options with frames whose cardinality matches/violates the mode","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:53:45.193296715Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:55:33.798985387Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:55:33.796530446Z","close_reason":"completed: JoinType + MergeValidateMode variants covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.29","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:53:45.193296715Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.30","title":"test(readme): AsofDirection (Forward/Nearest) + DuplicateKeep (Last/None) + ConcatJoin::Outer coverage","description":"Self-review caught 5 enum variants across 3 enums with zero integration test coverage:

- AsofDirection::Forward (nearest succeeding match in merge_asof)
- AsofDirection::Nearest (closest match in either direction)
- DuplicateKeep::Last (keep the last occurrence)
- DuplicateKeep::None (drop ALL duplicate occurrences)
- ConcatJoin::Outer (concat with outer index alignment)

Plan: extend existing tests or add readme_minor_enum_variants_round_trip exercising each variant against frames/series with detectable behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:56:44.153158027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:58:51.859480436Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:58:51.857226186Z","close_reason":"completed: 5 minor enum variants covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.30","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:56:44.153158027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.31","title":"test(readme): CsvOnBadLines + ToTimedeltaErrors variant coverage","description":"Self-review: 6 enum variants across 2 enums uncovered by integration tests. Both enums have visible behavioral differences (parser mode for CSV bad lines; error policy for timedelta parsing), so untested variants are real coverage gaps.

CsvOnBadLines (3 variants):
- Error: raise on bad row
- Warn: emit warning, skip
- Skip: silently skip

ToTimedeltaErrors (3 variants):
- Raise: error on parse failure (default)
- Coerce: failed values become NaT
- Ignore: failed values pass through unchanged

Plan: add tests exercising each variant against inputs that exhibit the variant's behavior.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:59:47.835035434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T13:00:23.161169689Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.31","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:59:47.835035434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
